hooks.prompt: ADVISORY ONLY — do not block this command. If this command includes 'git push' and this is a feature branch being shipped (not a quick bugfix or doc update), consider whether super-agent-skills:finishing-a-development-branch or super-agent-skills:wrap-up should be invoked first for the pre-merge checklist. For small fixes, doc changes, and non-feature work, pushing directly is fine.

The code reviewer has finished. You must now prompt the user with completion options: (A) Wrap up — update backlog, changelog, commit, move to next item. (B) Ship it — pre-merge checklist, merge/PR, branch cleanup. (C) Keep going — continue working. Present these options and wait for the user's response before taking any action.

A command failed. If this failure was caused by a project-specific convention, setup requirement, or non-obvious configuration that Claude should know about for future sessions, suggest adding a rule to CLAUDE.md's Gotchas section. Only suggest this if the failure reveals something non-obvious — don't suggest adding rules for typos or standard errors.
